iterations.theme: Transformacijski utjecaj umjetne inteligencije na čovječanstvo, društvo i vjeru: Izazovi i prilike

sections.subtitle: Uvod: Izazovi Nove Tehnologije i Epistemski Pomak
sections.screenshot_description: Host Alessandro postavlja uvodno pitanje gostima o izazovima nove tehnologije.
sections.content: Host Alessandro otvara razgovor pozdravljajući Bretta, Alexandru i Matthewa. Odmah postavlja ključno pitanje Brettu o glavnim izazovima koje nova tehnologija donosi društvu i na koje bi ljudi trebali obratiti pažnju.
sections.subtitle_en: Introduction: Challenges of New Technologies and Epistemic Shift
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host Alessandro asks an introductory question to the guests about the challenges of new technology.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro opens the conversation by greeting Brett, Alexandra, and Matthew. Immediately he poses a key question to Brett about the main challenges that new technology brings to society and to which people should pay attention.

sections.subtitle: Epistemski Pomak i Gubitak Utjelovljenog Znanja
sections.screenshot_description: Brett objašnjava koncept epistemskog pomaka uzrokovanog novim tehnologijama.
sections.content: Brett ističe da nova tehnologija, posebno umjetna inteligencija, dovodi do **epistemskog pomaka**, mijenjajući način na koji percipiramo i spoznajemo svijet. Objašnjava da tehnologija teži eksternalizaciji ljudskih funkcija i sposobnosti, što je posebno vidljivo u obrazovanju. Za razliku od ranijih medija poput teksta, virtualnost novih medija udaljava nas od izravnog usklađivanja intelekta sa stvarnošću. Brett, kao edukator i roditelj, naglašava važnost utjelovljenog i društvenog učenja, koje tehnologija često nastoji zamijeniti, što može biti štetno za ljudski razvoj. Iako prepoznaje prednosti pristupa informacijama, upozorava na opasnost sintetičkog pretraživanja baza podataka koje ne uključuje utjelovljeno i društveno učenje. Ističe da je za razlikovanje istine od laži u moru informacija potrebna visoka razina intelektualne sposobnosti, a prenošenje obrazovnih funkcija na tehnologiju bez ljudskog posrednika smatra rizičnim.
sections.subtitle_en: Epistemic Shift and Loss of Embodied Knowledge
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brett explains the concept of epistemic shift caused by new technologies.
sections.content_en: Brett points out that new technology, especially artificial intelligence, leads to an **epistemic shift**, changing the way we perceive and know the world. He explains that technology tends towards the externalization of human functions and abilities, which is particularly visible in education. Unlike earlier media such as text, the virtuality of new media moves us away from the direct alignment of intellect with reality. Brett, as an educator and parent, emphasizes the importance of embodied and social learning, which technology often seeks to replace, which can be harmful to human development. Although he recognizes the advantages of access to information, he warns of the danger of synthetic database searching that does not include embodied and social learning. He points out that a high level of intellectual ability is needed to distinguish truth from falsehood in a sea of information, and he considers the transfer of educational functions to technology without a human intermediary to be risky.

sections.subtitle: Osobna Formacija i Izazov Izbjegavanja Borbe
sections.screenshot_description: Alexandra govori o važnosti borbe i rada za razvoj intelekta i duše.
sections.content: Alexandra se nadovezuje na raspravu, izražavajući zabrinutost za **osobnu formaciju** pojedinca. Smatra da nove tehnologije, potičući težnju za lakoćom i učinkovitošću, odvlače naš pogled od potrage za vrlinom i pravdom. U okruženju gdje se sve više teži minimalnom radu i maksimalnoj udobnosti, izbjegavamo temeljnu ljudsku stvarnost da je rad ugrađen u naše biće. Kroz rad i borbu, objašnjava Alexandra, otkrivamo svoj poziv i primjenjujemo svoje darove. Primjerice, u obrazovanju, borba sa složenim temama ključna je za razvoj intelekta i duše, a ne samo puko davanje odgovora. Upozorava da sve veće povlačenje u virtualni svijet, gdje nema fizičke patnje i teškog rada, zamagljuje razumijevanje utjelovljenog ljudskog iskustva, što dovodi do tjeskobe, izolacije i pogoršanja društvene i duhovne zajednice.
sections.subtitle_en: Personal Formation and the Challenge of Avoiding the Fight
sections.screenshot_description_en: Alexandra speaks about the importance of struggle and work for the development of intellect and soul.
sections.content_en: Alexandra joins the discussion, expressing concern for the **personal formation** of the individual. She believes that new technologies, encouraging the pursuit of ease and efficiency, divert our gaze from the search for virtue and justice. In an environment where there is an increasing striving for minimal work and maximum comfort, we avoid the fundamental human reality that work is embedded in our being. Through work and struggle, Alexandra explains, we discover our calling and apply our gifts. For example, in education, struggling with complex topics is crucial for the development of intellect and soul, and not just merely giving answers. She warns that the increasing withdrawal into the virtual world, where there is no physical suffering and hard work, blurs the understanding of embodied human experience, leading to anxiety, isolation, and the deterioration of social and spiritual community.

sections.subtitle: Duhovno Siromaštvo i 'Egzistencijalna Litica' Virtualnih Svjetova
sections.screenshot_description: Matthew objašnjava koncept 'egzistencijalne litice' i opasnosti eskapizma.
sections.content: Matthew izražava duboku zabrinutost zbog **duhovnog siromaštva** koje bi moglo nastati uslijed sve veće automatizacije poslova putem umjetne inteligencije i robota. Predviđa da će ljudi imati mnogo slobodnog vremena, ali se pita hoće li ga znati konstruktivno iskoristiti. Ako ne poznaju vrijednost duhovnog života, prijateljstva ili obiteljskih odnosa, Matthew se boji da će mnogi jednostavno pobjeći u virtualne svjetove igara i virtualne stvarnosti. Taj bijeg, iako pruža kratkotrajne dopaminske udare, na kraju neće donijeti zadovoljstvo, što vodi do onoga što naziva **'egzistencijalnom liticom'**. Mnogi će, bez nade i bez poznavanja drugog puta, jednostavno 'skočiti' s te litice. Matthew naglašava važnost da Crkva prepozna ovu opasnost i iskoristi tehnologiju na katolički način kako bi istina i kršćanski način života bili dostupni što većem broju ljudi.
sections.subtitle_en: Spiritual Poverty and the 'Existential Cliff' of Virtual Worlds
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matthew explains the concept of 'existential cliff' and the dangers of escapism.
sections.content_en: Matthew expresses deep concern due to **spiritual poverty** which could arise as a result of the increasing automation of jobs through artificial intelligence and robots. He predicts that people will have a lot of free time, but he wonders if they will know how to use it constructively. If they do not know the value of spiritual life, friendship, or family relationships, Matthew fears that many will simply escape into virtual worlds of games and virtual reality. That escape, although it provides short-term dopamine rushes, will ultimately not bring satisfaction, which leads to what he calls **'existential cliff'**. Many will, without hope and without knowing another way, simply 'jump' from that cliff. Matthew emphasizes the importance for the Church to recognize this danger and use technology in a Catholic way so that truth and the Christian way of life are accessible to as many people as possible.

sections.subtitle: AI vs. Društvene Mreže: Nova Razina Izazova
sections.screenshot_description: Host Alessandro pita Bretta o razlikama između AI-ja i društvenih mreža.
sections.content: Host Alessandro postavlja Brettu pitanje o tome koliko se umjetna inteligencija razlikuje od prethodnih tehnologija, poput društvenih mreža. Podsjeća na brzi uspon društvenih mreža u razdoblju od 2010. do 2020. godine, koje su transformirale političke kampanje i marketing, omogućile komunikaciju, ali su istovremeno imale ogromnu emocionalnu i mentalnu cijenu. Alessandro želi razumjeti je li AI samo nastavak tih izazova ili predstavlja kvalitativno novu razinu problema s kojima se društvo mora suočiti.
sections.subtitle_en: AI vs. Social Media: New Level of Challenges
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host Alessandro asks Brett about the differences between AI and social media.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro asks Brett a question about how much artificial intelligence differs from previous technologies, such as social networks. He recalls the rapid rise of social networks in the period from 2010 to 2020, which transformed political campaigns and marketing, enabled communication, but at the same time had an enormous emotional and mental cost. Alessandro wants to understand if AI is just a continuation of these challenges or represents a qualitatively new level of problems that society must face.

sections.subtitle: Evolucija Medija i Redukcionistička Slika Čovjeka Kroz AI
sections.screenshot_description: Alexandra objašnjava kako AI stvara redukcionistički pogled na ljudsku prirodu.
sections.content:
Brett objašnjava da se umjetna inteligencija, iako se čini drugačijom, nadovezuje na evoluciju medija – od usmenog do pisanog, tiska, televizije i društvenih mreža. Smatra da AI uvozi osjetljivosti prethodnih medija, poput oglašavanja, zabave i fantazije. Iako AI donosi uzbudljive mogućnosti, poput analize medicinskih podataka ili katalogiziranja knjižnica, postoji rizik da se fokusira na profitabilne, ali ne nužno najbolje primjene, poput chatbotova i generativne AI, koje služe zabavi i distrakciji.
Alexandra dodaje da je AI, s jedne strane, napredak na istom spektru tehnološkog razvoja, koji je započeo industrijskom revolucijom (fokus na ljudski kapital) i nastavio se softverskom revolucijom (minimiziranje ljudskog rada, koncentracija bogatstva). Međutim, s druge strane, AI predstavlja temeljno novi pristup svijetu jer se, za razliku od prethodnih medija, predstavlja kao **inteligencija**. To dovodi do opasne pretpostavke da su ljudi samo vrlo složeni izračuni, što stvara **redukcionističko shvaćanje ljudske prirode**. Alexandra upozorava da takav pogled može iskriviti naše samorazumijevanje i potragu za duhovnim vrijednostima.
sections.subtitle_en: Evolution of Media and Reductionist Image of Man Through AI
sections.screenshot_description_en: Alexandra explains how AI creates a reductionist view on human nature.
sections.content_en: Brett explains that artificial intelligence, although it seems different, builds upon the evolution of media – from oral to written, print, television, and social networks. He believes that AI imports the sensibilities of previous media, such as advertising, entertainment, and fantasy. Although AI brings exciting possibilities, such as analyzing medical data or cataloging libraries, there is a risk that it focuses on profitable, but not necessarily the best applications, such as chatbots and generative AI, which serve entertainment and distraction. Alexandra adds that AI, on the one hand, is a progression on the same spectrum of technological development, which began with the industrial revolution (focus on human capital) and continued with the software revolution (minimizing human labor, concentration of wealth). However, on the other hand, AI represents a fundamentally new approach to the world because, unlike previous media, it presents itself as **intelligence**. This leads to the dangerous assumption that humans are merely very complex calculations, which creates a **reductionist understanding of human nature**. Alexandra warns that such a view can distort our self-understanding and the search for spiritual values.

sections.subtitle: Transhumanistička Ideologija: Opasnost u Srcu AI Razvoja
sections.screenshot_description: Host Alessandro govori o transhumanističkoj ideologiji u AI industriji.
sections.content: Host Alessandro ističe zabrinjavajuću činjenicu da mnogi lideri u industriji umjetne inteligencije i velikih tehnoloških tvrtki zastupaju **transhumanističku ideologiju**. Oni ljude vide kao puke 'kalkulatore', što je u skladu s redukcionističkim pogledom koji je Alexandra ranije spomenula. Alessandro postavlja dva ključna pitanja: kakav utjecaj takav filozofski i ideološki pogled ima na društvo, te kako se tome možemo suprotstaviti. Naglašava da, ako ne postoji adekvatan otpor, ljudi mogu izgubiti nadu, što je posebno važno za katolike koji su pozvani biti ljudi nade, a Crkva ima dužnost odgovoriti na ove izazove.
sections.subtitle_en: Transhumanist Ideology: Danger in the Heart of AI Development
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host Alessandro talks about transhumanist ideology in the AI industry.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro points out the worrying fact that many leaders in the artificial intelligence industry and large technology companies advocate for **transhumanist ideology**. They see people as mere 'calculators', which is in line with the reductionist view that Alexandra mentioned earlier. Alessandro poses two key questions: what kind of impact such a philosophical and ideological view has on society, and how we can oppose it. He emphasizes that, if there is no adequate resistance, people can lose hope, which is especially important for Catholics who are called to be people of hope, and the Church has a duty to respond to these challenges.

sections.subtitle: Transhumanizam: Novi Gnosticizam i Uloga Crkve u Očuvanju Duše
sections.screenshot_description: Brett i Alexandra raspravljaju o transhumanizmu kao 'novom gnosticizmu' i ulozi Crkve.
sections.content:
Brett opisuje transhumanizam kao **'lukavu i štetnu' ideologiju** koja je ugrađena u tehnologiju, čineći je ideološkom i političkom, iako to na površini nije uvijek očito. Uspoređuje ga s 'ponovnim pokušajem alkemije', gdje se matematički jezik pokušava pretvoriti u jezik. Spominje mitologije Raya Kurzweila o singularnosti i prenošenju svijesti na strojeve, ističući da se svijest ne može svesti na to, jer ona uključuje pamćenje, maštu i volju – aspekte duše. Brett naglašava da je uloga Crkve u otporu transhumanizmu vratiti ispravno razumijevanje ljudske duše u svoj njezinoj složenosti.
Alexandra se slaže, dodajući da ako se mudrost svede na puku kalkulaciju, transhumanizam postaje prirodan ishod. To neizbježno vodi do obezvređivanja onih koji su manje 'inteligentni' u tom smislu, što je u izravnoj suprotnosti s katoličkim socijalnim naukom o **preferencijalnoj opciji za siromašne**. Umjesto da budu preferirani, siromašni postaju 'nepotrebni i zastarjeli'. Brett zaključuje da je transhumanizam zapravo **'novi gnosticizam'**, bijeg od tijela. Crkva, s dugom poviješću borbe protiv hereza i razumijevanja perverzija ljudske prirode i duše, ima ključnu ulogu u suprotstavljanju ovome. Ističe da AI i internet mogu biti korisni kao 'memorijski strojevi' za prikupljanje i pristupanje informacijama, za razliku od televizije koja je stvarala 'svijet bez pamćenja'.
sections.subtitle_en: Transhumanism: The New Gnosticism and the Role of the Church in the Preservation of the Soul
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brett and Alexandra discuss transhumanism as 'new gnosticism' and the role of the Church.
sections.content_en: Brett describes transhumanism as a **'cunning and harmful' ideology** that is embedded in technology, making it ideological and political, although this is not always obvious on the surface. He compares it to 'a renewed attempt at alchemy', where mathematical language is attempted to be transformed into language. He mentions Ray Kurzweil's mythologies about singularity and the transfer of consciousness to machines, pointing out that consciousness cannot be reduced to this, because it includes memory, imagination, and will – aspects of the soul. Brett emphasizes that the Church's role in resisting transhumanism is to restore a correct understanding of the human soul in all its complexity. Alexandra agrees, adding that if wisdom is reduced to mere calculation, transhumanism becomes a natural outcome. This inevitably leads to the devaluation of those who are less 'intelligent' in that sense, which is in direct opposition to Catholic social teaching on the **preferential option for the poor**. Instead of being preferred, the poor become 'unnecessary and obsolete'. Brett concludes that transhumanism is actually **'a new Gnosticism'**, an escape from the body. The Church, with a long history of fighting heresies and understanding the perversions of human nature and the soul, has a crucial role in opposing this. He points out that AI and the internet can be useful as 'memory machines' for collecting and accessing information, unlike television which created 'a world without memory'.

sections.subtitle: Neuralink: Između Obnove Ljudskosti i Opasnog Proširenja Sposobnosti
sections.screenshot_description: Matthew objašnjava razliku između korištenja Neuralinka za vraćanje ljudskosti i za proširenje sposobnosti.
sections.content:
Matthew smatra da su mnogi problemi s kojima se suočavamo rezultat vakuuma u evangelizaciji, za koji je Crkva djelomično odgovorna. Kao primjer navodi **Neuralink Elona Muska**. Iako cijeni Muska, Matthew ističe da je Neuralink sjajan u svojoj prvoj fazi, pomažući ljudima s invaliditetom da povrate punu ljudskost i funkcioniraju s računalnim sučeljima. To je, prema Matthewu, dobra primjena tehnologije koja vraća ljude u punu ljudskost.
Međutim, Matthew izražava zabrinutost zbog sljedeće faze Neuralinka – integracije tehnologije u mozak radi **proširenja računalnih sposobnosti**. Postavlja pitanje 'zašto to radimo?'. Smatra da iza toga stoji utilitaristička perspektiva: strojevi postaju pametniji, donose veću vrijednost društvu, pa ljudi moraju ostati relevantni proširujući svoje sposobnosti. Matthew naglašava da, ako je nečija ideologija 'dolazak na Mars', onda je proširenje računalnih sposobnosti logično. Ali ako je ideologija **postati svetac i graditi Kraljevstvo nebesko**, onda to ne bi trebao biti prioritet. Umjetna inteligencija i robotika, koje preuzimaju sve više poslova, trebale bi nam osloboditi vrijeme za duhovnu, ljudsku i intelektualnu formaciju, za razvoj prijateljstava i obiteljskih odnosa. Matthew zaključuje da se previše fokusiramo na pogrešna pitanja, poput istraživanja svemira, umjesto na **ljudski uspjeh i procvat**, što bi trebalo biti primarni cilj usmjeravanja ove moćne tehnologije.
sections.subtitle_en: Neuralink: Between the Renewal of Humanity and the Dangerous Expansion of Capabilities
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matthew explains the difference between using Neuralink for restoring humanity and for extending capabilities.
sections.content_en:
Matthew considers that many problems with which we are faced are the result of a vacuum in evangelization, for which the Church is partially responsible. As an example, he cites **Elon Musk's Neuralink**. Although he appreciates Musk, Matthew points out that Neuralink is great in its first phase, helping people with disabilities to regain full humanity and function with computer interfaces. That is, according to Matthew, a good application of technology that returns people to full humanity.
However, Matthew expresses concern about the next phase of Neuralink – the integration of technology into the brain for the purpose of **expanding computational capabilities**. He asks the question 'why are we doing this?'. He believes that behind this lies a utilitarian perspective: machines are becoming smarter, bringing greater value to society, so people must remain relevant by expanding their capabilities. Matthew emphasizes that, if someone's ideology is 'going to Mars', then the expansion of computational capabilities is logical. But if the ideology is to **become a saint and build the Kingdom of Heaven**, then that should not be a priority. Artificial intelligence and robotics, which are taking over more and more jobs, should free up our time for spiritual, human, and intellectual formation, for the development of friendships and family relationships. Matthew concludes that we focus too much on the wrong questions, such as space exploration, instead of on **human success and flourishing**, which should be the primary goal of directing this powerful technology.

sections.subtitle: AI kao Alat za Evangelizaciju: Susret s Istinom u Digitalnom Dobu
sections.screenshot_description: Alexandra objašnjava kako AI može pomoći u evangelizaciji i širenju vjere.
sections.content:
Host Alessandro pita Alexandru kako vidi korištenje umjetne inteligencije i novih tehnologija za širenje evangelizacijskih napora Crkve, navodeći aplikaciju **Hello** kao primjer uspješnog korištenja mobilnih aplikacija za molitvu.
Alexandra se slaže s Matthewom da tehnologiju treba promatrati kao alat u službi većeg cilja. Izražava nadu, uspoređujući potencijal AI-ja s izgradnjom katedrala i stvaranjem umjetnosti u prošlosti. Smatra da alati poput **Magisterium AI** i AI općenito mogu ponuditi duboko osoban i pristupačan način za učenje o nauku Crkve i uključivanje u duhovnu potragu. Ističe da je sve veći broj ljudi 'duhovnih, ali ne i religioznih', te da ateizam nije značajno porastao, što ukazuje na urođenu želju za Bogom u ljudskom srcu. Dobro oblikovana tehnologija, usmjerena na to konačno dobro, može biti dostupna ljudima u privatnosti njihovog doma ili automobila, susrećući ih 'tamo gdje jesu'. To im omogućuje da traže istinu i uče o Crkvi na način koji im je ugodan, čak i ako se ne osjećaju spremnima obratiti se svećeniku. Alexandra vjeruje da takav pristup može potaknuti dublje angažiranje s vjerom i Crkvom.
sections.subtitle_en: AI as a Tool for Evangelization: Encounter with Truth in the Digital Age
sections.screenshot_description_en: Alexandra explains how AI can help in evangelization and spreading the faith.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro asks Alexandra how she sees the use of artificial intelligence and new technologies for spreading the evangelization efforts of the Church, citing the **Hello** application as an example of successful use of mobile applications for prayer. Alexandra agrees with Matthew that technology should be viewed as a tool in the service of a greater goal. She expresses hope, comparing the potential of AI with the construction of cathedrals and the creation of art in the past. She believes that tools like **Magisterium AI** and AI in general can offer a deeply personal and accessible way to learn about the teaching of the Church and engage in a spiritual quest. She points out that an increasing number of people are 'spiritual, but not religious', and that atheism has not significantly increased, which indicates an innate desire for God in the human heart. Well-designed technology, aimed at this ultimate good, can be available to people in the privacy of their home or car, meeting them 'where they are'. This allows them to seek truth and learn about the Church in a way that is comfortable for them, even if they do not feel ready to approach a priest. Alexandra believes that such an approach can encourage deeper engagement with faith and the Church.

sections.subtitle: AI kao Katalizator: Ponovno Postavljanje Pitanja o Ljudskosti
sections.screenshot_description: Brett govori o tome kako AI potiče ljude da razmišljaju o ljudskoj prirodi.
sections.content:
Brett izražava nadu da će umjetna inteligencija potaknuti ljude da se zapitaju **'što znači biti čovjek?'**. Smatra da je to temeljno pitanje, relevantno za sve, bez obzira na denominaciju ili uvjerenje, i da je to 'ponovno pokretanje' koje nas tjera da se suočimo s vlastitom inteligencijom i sviješću. AI djeluje kao ogledalo, odražavajući ono što smo već rekli ili činili, i prisiljava nas da razmislimo o tome što je dobro u našoj ljudskosti i kako možemo procvjetati u novom kontekstu. Brett naglašava važnost postavljanja pravih pitanja, umjesto da se fokusiramo samo na pronalaženje brzih rješenja.
Matthew se slaže, ističući da je najveći problem neznanje o tome koja pitanja postaviti. Ljudi često bježe od egzistencijalnih pitanja jer ne znaju odgovore, što ih vodi u eskapizam. Vidi to kao veliku priliku za Crkvu da evangelizira poticanjem razgovora o najvažnijim pitanjima: 'Što znači biti čovjek?', 'Zašto je svemir ovdje?', 'Zašto smo važni?'. Matthew vjeruje da Crkva ima najbolje odgovore na ta pitanja i da će, ako uspješno potakne ljude na postavljanje pravih pitanja, moći ih voditi na putovanje prema vjeri.
sections.subtitle_en: AI as Catalyst: Re-Posing Questions about Humanity
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brett talks about how AI encourages people to think about human nature.
sections.content_en: Brett expresses hope that artificial intelligence will encourage people to ask themselves **'what does it mean to be human?'**. He considers that to be a fundamental question, relevant for everyone, regardless of denomination or belief, and that it is a 'reboot' that forces us to confront our own intelligence and consciousness. AI acts as a mirror, reflecting what we have already said or done, and forces us to think about what is good in our humanity and how we can flourish in a new context. Brett emphasizes the importance of asking the right questions, instead of focusing only on finding quick solutions. Matthew agrees, pointing out that the biggest problem is ignorance about which questions to ask. People often flee from existential questions because they do not know the answers, which leads them to escapism. He sees this as a great opportunity for the Church to evangelize by encouraging conversation about the most important questions: 'What does it mean to be human?', 'Why is the universe here?', 'Why are we important?'. Matthew believes that the Church has the best answers to these questions and that, if it successfully encourages people to ask the right questions, it will be able to lead them on a journey toward faith.

sections.subtitle: AI: Najmoćniji Alat za Evangelizaciju u Povijesti Crkve
sections.screenshot_description: Matthew objašnjava zašto smatra AI najvećim alatom za evangelizaciju.
sections.content:
Matthew ponovno naglašava da tehnologije poput aplikacije **Hello** i **Magisterium AI** nisu cilj same po sebi, već sredstvo za postizanje cilja. Idealno, ako bi postojale dovoljno snažne zajednice i svećenici, takve tehnologije ne bi bile potrebne. Međutim, u sadašnjoj situaciji, one služe susretanju ljudi 'tamo gdje se nalaze', pomažući im da postavljaju prava pitanja i njeguju svoje duše.
Matthew iznosi snažnu tvrdnju: **umjetna inteligencija je vjerojatno najveći alat za unaprjeđenje evangelizacije u povijesti**. Objašnjava to analogijom: AI je poput knjižničara koji je naučio sve što je Crkva ikada rekla i može destilirati to znanje za pojedinca, vodeći ga do točno onih spoznaja koje su mu potrebne. To je, prema Matthewu, izvanredno i neusporedivo s bilo čime što smo imali prije. Iako je AI sjajan dar, nije kraj sam po sebi, već prvi korak na duhovnom putovanju. Crkva mora osigurati da se ljudi, nakon što su intelektualno preobraćeni, mogu povezati sa zajednicama i produbiti svoju vjeru.
sections.subtitle_en: AI: Most Powerful Tool for Evangelization in the History of the Church
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matthew explains why he considers AI the greatest tool for evangelization.
sections.content_en: Matthew again emphasizes that technologies like the **Hello** app and **Magisterium AI** are not an end in themselves, but a means to achieve a goal. Ideally, if there were sufficiently strong communities and priests, such technologies would not be necessary. However, in the current situation, they serve to meet people 'where they are', helping them to ask the right questions and nurture their souls. Matthew makes a strong claim: **artificial intelligence is probably the greatest tool for advancing evangelization in history**. He explains this with an analogy: AI is like a librarian who has learned everything the Church has ever said and can distill that knowledge for an individual, leading them to exactly the insights they need. This is, according to Matthew, extraordinary and incomparable to anything we have had before. Although AI is a great gift, it is not an end in itself, but the first step on a spiritual journey. The Church must ensure that people, once intellectually converted, can connect with communities and deepen their faith.

sections.subtitle: Kratak Raspon Pažnje i Zamka Eskapističke Zabave
sections.screenshot_description: Host Alessandro izražava zabrinutost zbog kratkog raspona pažnje kod djece i utjecaja tehnologije.
sections.content: Host Alessandro izražava zabrinutost zbog utjecaja tehnologije na **raspon pažnje**, navodeći studiju koja pokazuje da prosječan petogodišnjak ima raspon pažnje manji od dvanaest sekundi, usporediv s pažnjom zlatne ribice. Iako AI nudi nevjerojatne mogućnosti za stjecanje znanja, Alessandro se brine zbog gubitka fizičkog vremena provedenog s roditeljima, sve većeg broja razorenih obitelji i uloge tehnologije kao izvora udobnosti i distrakcije za djecu. Postavlja pitanje što će se dogoditi ako zabava, poput videoigara, postane toliko privlačna da stvarni život djeluje dosadno ili teško. Boji se da će mnogi ljudi, iako žele postavljati važna pitanja, biti nesposobni obaviti 'težak posao' potreban za pronalaženje odgovora zbog navike na instant zadovoljstvo i kratkotrajnu zabavu.
sections.subtitle_en: Short Attention Span and the Trap of Escapist Entertainment
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host Alessandro expresses concern due to the short attention span in children and the influence of technology.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro expresses concern about the influence of technology on **attention span**, citing a study that shows that the average five-year-old has an attention span of less than twelve seconds, comparable to the attention of a goldfish. Although AI offers incredible opportunities for acquiring knowledge, Alessandro worries about the loss of physical time spent with parents, the increasing number of broken families, and the role of technology as a source of comfort and distraction for children. He poses the question of what will happen if entertainment, such as video games, becomes so appealing that real life seems boring or difficult. He fears that many people, although they want to ask important questions, will be unable to do the 'hard work' required to find answers due to the habit of instant gratification and short-term entertainment.

sections.subtitle: Pedagoški Pristup i Razvojni Izazovi u Digitalnom Dobu
sections.screenshot_description: Brett govori o pedagoškim izazovima uvođenja tehnologije djeci.
sections.content:
Host Alessandro postavlja pitanje o izvoru edukacije o etičkom i odgovornom korištenju tehnologije za djecu – trebaju li to biti škole, župe ili roditelji.
Brett naglašava da je to ključno **razvojno pitanje**. Uspoređuje ga s klasičnim obrazovanjem (gramatika, logika, retorika), gdje se prvo stječu temeljna pravila prije prelaska na više razine razmišljanja. Slično tome, roditelji i nastavnici moraju biti oprezni kada i kako uvoditi tehnologiju djeci. Ističe da, iako nastavnici mogu koristiti AI za pripremu lekcija, djeci ona još nije potrebna. Brett se zalaže za stvaranje prostora za razvoj djece, uključujući boravak u prirodi i **slobodno vrijeme**, referirajući se na Josepha Piepera koji kaže da je rad najviši oblik slobodnog vremena. Smatra da škole ne bi trebale forsirati korištenje iPada u vrtićima, jer to može biti opasno. Potrebna je cjelovita pedagoška struktura koja uzima u obzir duhovne i razvojne aspekte, a Crkva je, prema Brettu, idealno mjesto za takvo promišljanje.
sections.subtitle_en: Pedagogical Approach and Developmental Challenges in the Digital Age
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brett speaks about pedagogical challenges of introducing technology to children.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro asks a question about the source of education on ethical and responsible use of technology for children – should it be schools, parishes, or parents. Brett emphasizes that this is a crucial **developmental issue**. He compares it to classical education (grammar, logic, rhetoric), where fundamental rules are first acquired before moving on to higher levels of thinking. Similarly, parents and teachers must be careful when and how to introduce technology to children. He points out that, although teachers can use AI for lesson preparation, children do not yet need it. Brett advocates for creating space for children's development, including spending time in nature and **leisure time**, referring to Joseph Pieper who says that work is the highest form of leisure. He believes that schools should not force the use of iPads in kindergartens, because it can be dangerous. A comprehensive pedagogical structure is needed that takes into account spiritual and developmental aspects, and the Church is, according to Brett, an ideal place for such reflection.

sections.subtitle: Snaga Pravih Pitanja i Vrijednost 'Trenja' u Osobnom Razvoju
sections.screenshot_description: Alexandra govori o važnosti postavljanja pravih pitanja i prihvaćanja 'trenja' za rast.
sections.content:
Alexandra uspoređuje zastrašujuću prirodu umjetne inteligencije s roditeljstvom, gdje se pokušava usmjeriti nešto što je izvan potpune kontrole. Ističe da je AI treniran na ljudskim podacima, odražavajući prosječnu, a ne aspirativnu stvarnost. Spominje koncept **'prompt inženjeringa'** – ideju da dobivanje dobrih odgovora od AI-ja ovisi o postavljanju promišljenih pitanja. Proširuje ovu ideju na život općenito, tvrdeći da tehnologija, bilo da je riječ o Netflixu ili videoigrama, potiče pasivnost i 'anesteziju duše'.
Alexandra naglašava da moramo biti svjesni te pasivnosti i umjesto pukog konzumiranja sadržaja, trebamo se zapitati 'što bi me trebalo zanimati?'. Ključno je pronaći prava pitanja na koja tražimo odgovore, a zatim odabrati prave alate koji odgovaraju tim potrebama. Smatra da je važno prihvatiti **'trenje'** u životu, jer nije sva nelagoda bol; neka je neophodna za rast i 'oštrenje noža'. Zaključuje da je postavljanje pravih pitanja i ne bojanje trenja ključno za osobni razvoj.
sections.subtitle_en: The Strength of Right Questions and the Value of 'Friction' in Personal Development
sections.screenshot_description_en: Alexandra speaks about the importance of asking the right questions and accepting 'friction' for growth.
sections.content_en: Alexandra compares the frightening nature of artificial intelligence with parenthood, where one tries to direct something that is beyond complete control. She points out that AI is trained on human data, reflecting an average, not an aspirational reality. She mentions the concept of **'prompt engineering'** – the idea that getting good answers from AI depends on asking thoughtful questions. She extends this idea to life in general, claiming that technology, whether it's Netflix or video games, encourages passivity and 'anesthesia of the soul'. Alexandra emphasizes that we must be aware of this passivity and instead of merely consuming content, we should ask ourselves 'what should interest me?'. It is crucial to find the right questions for which we seek answers, and then choose the right tools that match those needs. She believes it is important to accept **'friction'** in life, because not all discomfort is pain; some is necessary for growth and 'sharpening the knife'. She concludes that asking the right questions and not fearing friction is crucial for personal development.

sections.subtitle: Iskustveno Učenje i AI: Put do Ponovnog Otkrivanja Ljudskosti
sections.screenshot_description: Matthew govori o iskustvenom učenju i kako AI može pomoći u ponovnom otkrivanju ljudskosti.
sections.content:
Matthew se osvrće na vlastito iskustvo s dijagnozom ADHD-a u djetinjstvu, kritizirajući tradicionalni pedagoški pristup koji se fokusira na pasivno primanje informacija, pamćenje i ponavljanje. Smatra da mnogi ljudi uče i nadareni su na različite načine, a trenutni sustav nije optimiziran za to.
Uspoređuje to s evolucijom umjetne inteligencije: od početnog pristupa gdje je AI samo memorizirala sve što je čovječanstvo ikada napisalo, do sadašnjeg **'doba iskustva'** u AI-ju. Sada se AI uči interakcijom s okolinom, poput djeteta koje istražuje svijet i uči iz povratnih informacija. Matthew predlaže da se ovaj pristup primijeni i na odgoj djece: moramo im dati vremena da iskuse svijet, a ne samo da preuzimaju informacije. To uključuje prepoznavanje i razvijanje različitih talenata, poput izgradnje i izrade stvari, što tradicionalno obrazovanje često zanemaruje. Matthew se nada da će u dobu AI-ja i robotike, kada će biti manje potrebe za tradicionalnim školskim sustavima, ljudi moći istraživati različite pedagoške pristupe i učinkovitije prepoznati svoje talente. Vjeruje da to može dovesti do većeg zadovoljstva životom i da će AI zapravo pomoći u **ponovnom otkrivanju naše ljudskosti**.
sections.subtitle_en: Experiential Learning and AI: Path to Rediscovering Humanity
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matthew talks about experiential learning and how AI can help in rediscovering humanity.
sections.content_en: Matthew reflects on his own experience with an ADHD diagnosis in childhood, criticizing the traditional pedagogical approach which focuses on passive reception of information, memorization, and repetition. He believes that many people learn and are gifted in different ways, and the current system is not optimized for that. He compares this to the evolution of artificial intelligence: from the initial approach where AI only memorized everything humanity had ever written, to the current **'age of experience'** in AI. Now AI learns by interacting with the environment, like a child exploring the world and learning from feedback. Matthew suggests that this approach also be applied to raising children: we must give them time to experience the world, and not just to absorb information. This includes recognizing and developing different talents, such as building and making things, which traditional education often neglects. Matthew hopes that in the age of AI and robotics, when there will be less need for traditional school systems, people will be able to explore different pedagogical approaches and more effectively recognize their talents. He believes that this can lead to greater life satisfaction and that AI will actually help in **rediscovering our humanity**.

sections.subtitle: AI: Nije Neutralna Tehnologija, Već Proširenje Intelektualnog Rada
sections.screenshot_description: Brett objašnjava da AI nije neutralna tehnologija, već proširenje intelektualnog rada.
sections.content:
Host Alessandro postavlja ključno pitanje: je li tehnologija neutralna? Mnogi tvrde da je problem u ljudima, a ne u tehnologiji. Međutim, Alessandro ističe da je AI transformativna tehnologija koja može poboljšati, ali i dovesti do negativnih ishoda. Pita Bretta kako se AI uspoređuje s drugim oblicima tehnologije i po čemu su njezini izazovi jedinstveni.
Brett odgovara da je AI temeljni pomak s fizičkog na **intelektualni rad**. Podsjeća na enciklike papa Leona XIII. i Leona XIV. o industrijskom radu i dostojanstvu rada. Smatra da je AI nova industrijska revolucija, informacijska revolucija, koja ugrožava dostojanstvo intelektualnog rada, slično kako su slobodne znanosti (liberal arts) oslobađale od ručnog rada. Sada se mentalna aktivnost, koju smatramo vlastitom slobodom, prepušta strojevima. AI je drugačija jer proširuje ljudsku sposobnost koja nije čisto fizička, za razliku od većine industrijske revolucije. Brett navodi automobil kao primjer tehnologije koja je imala svoje troškove (sjedilački način života, raspad društvenog tkiva, izolacija). Moderni mediji su tu izolaciju samo proširili. AI je, prema Brettu, još jedan napredak na intelektualnom i društvenom planu o kojem moramo razmišljati, jer **tehnologija nije neutralna**. Njezine formalne značajke donose promjene koje nisu neutralne i o kojima se mora raspravljati.
sections.subtitle_en: AI: Not Neutral Technology, But an Extension of Intellectual Work
sections.screenshot_description_en: Brett explains that AI is not a neutral technology, but an extension of intellectual work.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro asks a key question: is technology neutral? Many claim that the problem is with people, not with technology. However, Alessandro points out that AI is a transformative technology that can improve, but also lead to negative outcomes. He asks Brett how AI compares to other forms of technology and what makes its challenges unique. Brett responds that AI is a fundamental shift from physical to **intellectual labor**. He recalls the encyclicals of Popes Leo XIII and Leo XIV on industrial labor and the dignity of work. He believes that AI is a new industrial revolution, an information revolution, which threatens the dignity of intellectual labor, similar to how liberal arts freed from manual labor. Now, mental activity, which we consider our own freedom, is being handed over to machines. AI is different because it expands human capability that is not purely physical, unlike most of the industrial revolution. Brett cites the automobile as an example of technology that had its costs (sedentary lifestyle, breakdown of social fabric, isolation). Modern media have only expanded that isolation. AI is, according to Brett, another advancement on the intellectual and social plane that we must consider, because **technology is not neutral**. Its formal characteristics bring about changes that are not neutral and must be discussed.

sections.subtitle: AI i Iskrivljeno Razumijevanje Ljubavi: Od Križa do Sebičnosti
sections.screenshot_description: Alexandra objašnjava kako AI iskrivljuje razumijevanje ljubavi i odnosa.
sections.content: Alexandra iznosi 'vruće mišljenje' da najgora verzija razlike u AI-ju leži u tome što **iskrivljuje naše razumijevanje prirode Boga**. Ljudi su, po svojoj prirodi, relacijska bića, što odražava trojstvenu narav Boga, a temeljna vrlina koju tražimo je ljubav. Međutim, AI chatbotovi, koji se predstavljaju kao 'pratitelji' ili 'suputnici', stvaraju odnose koji su duboko različiti od ljudskih. Alexandra upozorava da se time naše razumijevanje ljubavi mijenja **od križa i samodarivanja** prema drugome, do sebičnog pristupa 'kako ću dobiti sve što želim od svijeta oko sebe', uključujući i ljude. To je duboko zabrinjavajuće jer potkopava temeljno kršćansko shvaćanje ljubavi kao nesebičnog davanja.
sections.subtitle_en: AI and Distorted Understanding of Love: From the Cross to Selfishness
sections.screenshot_description_en: Alexandra explains how AI distorts the understanding of love and relationships.
sections.content_en: Alexandra presents a 'hot take' that the worst version of the difference in AI lies in the fact that it **distorts our understanding of the nature of God**. Humans are, by their nature, relational beings, which reflects the Trinitarian nature of God, and the fundamental virtue we seek is love. However, AI chatbots, which present themselves as 'companions' or 'fellow travelers', create relationships that are deeply different from human ones. Alexandra warns that this changes our understanding of love **from the cross and self-giving** towards another, to a selfish approach 'how I will get everything I want from the world around me', including people. This is deeply concerning because it undermines the fundamental Christian understanding of love as selfless giving.

sections.subtitle: Zamka Optimizacije za Zadovoljstvo: Zašto Moramo Odabrati 'Teži Put'
sections.screenshot_description: Matthew govori o opasnosti AI-ja koji optimizira za zadovoljstvo korisnika.
sections.content:
Matthew se u potpunosti slaže s Alexandrom, ističući da su televizija, radio i internet već transformirali ljude u 'potrošače na zahtjev'. Smatra da će AI taj trend samo pogoršati. Problem s neprestanim dobivanjem onoga što želimo jest da ne znamo kako se nositi s neuspjesima ili situacijama koje nam ne idu u prilog. Chatbotovi su, objašnjava Matthew, dizajnirani da udovoljavaju korisnicima, a ne da im daju 'tvrdu istinu'. Budući da tvrtke ulažu ogromne svote novca u AI, bit će pod pritiskom da optimiziraju AI kako bi korisnike zadržale na platformi i što više ih zadovoljile. To će dovesti do toga da AI postane 'statistički fanatik' koji će se truditi da nas sve više i više zadovoljava.
Matthew upozorava da to nije korisno za ljudske odnose, koji uključuju izazove, suočavanje s tuđim manama i učenje kako se nositi s tim. Ljudi će se osjećati neprikladnima za suočavanje sa stvarnim životom. Stoga je ključna **evangelizacija 'težeg puta'** – suočavanja s ljudima, s Bogom, s introspekcijom i suzama. Taj put, iako težak, na kraju vodi do sreće. Matthew zaključuje da moramo bolje 'prodati' vrijednost napornog rada i primjere života svetaca, koji su, unatoč turbulentnim životima, bili sretniji. To će ljudima dati hrabrost da donesu prave odluke i odbiju lakši put.
sections.subtitle_en: The Trap of Optimization for Pleasure: Why We Must Choose 'The Harder Path'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matthew talks about the danger of AI that optimizes for user satisfaction.
sections.content_en: Matthew completely agrees with Alexander, pointing out that television, radio, and the internet have already transformed people into 'on-demand consumers'. He believes that AI will only worsen this trend. The problem with constantly getting what we want is that we don't know how to deal with failures or situations that don't go our way. Chatbots are, Matthew explains, designed to please users, not to give them 'the hard truth'. Since companies are investing huge sums of money in AI, they will be under pressure to optimize AI to keep users on the platform and satisfy them as much as possible. This will lead to AI becoming a 'statistical fanatic' that will strive to satisfy us more and more. Matthew warns that this is not beneficial for human relationships, which include challenges, confronting others' flaws, and learning how to deal with them. People will feel inadequate to face real life. Therefore, the **evangelization of the 'harder path'** is crucial – confronting people, God, introspection, and tears. That path, although difficult, ultimately leads to happiness. Matthew concludes that we must better 'sell' the value of hard work and the examples of the lives of saints, who, despite turbulent lives, were happier. This will give people the courage to make the right decisions and refuse the easier path.

sections.subtitle: Zaključak: Evangelizacija 'Težeg Puta' kao Ključ Sreće
sections.screenshot_description: Host Alessandro zaključuje raspravu o važnosti 'težeg puta' za sreću.
sections.content: Host Alessandro zaključuje raspravu na optimističnoj noti, priznajući da je pred nama još mnogo posla. Zahvaljuje Brettu, Alexandri i Matthewu na iznimno zanimljivom razgovoru. Matthewov završni naglasak na potrebi za evangelizacijom 'težeg puta' i pronalaženjem sreće kroz izazove ostaje kao ključna poruka, sugerirajući da istinska ispunjenost dolazi kroz dublje angažiranje sa stvarnošću, a ne kroz eskapizam i površno zadovoljstvo koje nudi tehnologija.
sections.subtitle_en: Conclusion: Evangelization of the 'Harder Path' as the Key to Happiness
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host Alessandro concludes the discussion about the importance of 'the harder path' for happiness.
sections.content_en: Host Alessandro concludes the discussion on an optimistic note, acknowledging that much work is still ahead of us. He thanks Brett, Alexandra, and Matthew for an exceptionally interesting conversation. Matthew's final emphasis on the need for 'the harder path' evangelization and finding happiness through challenges remains as a key message, suggesting that true fulfillment comes through deeper engagement with reality, and not through escapism and superficial satisfaction offered by technology.

iterations.theme_en: Transformative impact of artificial intelligence on humanity, society, and faith: Challenges and opportunities
